name: Northern Ireland (Saint Patrick's Saltire, alternative)
description: A white field with a red diagonal cross (saltire) extending corner to corner.
history: Adopted as the symbol of Saint Patrick by the Order of St Patrick in 1783; one of the three crosses combined into the modern Union Jack in 1801. Sometimes used as a less politically-charged alternative to the Ulster Banner for Northern Ireland.

features.symbolism: the diagonal cross of Saint Patrick — patron saint of Ireland — used as a more inclusive symbol than the Ulster Banner
